D9 Biology L4 Analyze Reference

Anglerfish Lure — Photobacterium Symbiosis

deep-sea anglerfish do not glow themselves — they house Photobacterium bacteria in a lure organ; the fish feeds them and the bacteria produce continuous blue light to lure prey

The female deep-sea anglerfish (Linophrynidae, Ceratiidae) carries an escal lure tipped with millions of Photobacterium phosphoreum bacteria. The fish supplies oxygen, nutrients and a controlled environment; the bacteria continuously produce 480 nm blue light from FMNH₂ + O₂ + long-chain aldehyde (luciferase reaction). The fish can dim the lure by restricting oxygen flow.

Drag the O₂ supply slider. Without O₂ the bacteria cannot run the reaction; the lure dims. The bacteria stay alive (they don't depend on the light for energy).
